name: Ground rod IS the bond
statement: The neutral-to-ground bond happens where the ground rod connects — the rod is the bond.
correction: The bond and the ground rod happen at the same place (the service disconnect) but they are different things. The bond is the main bonding jumper — a screw or strap inside the panel that ties the neutral bus to the EGC bus. The ground rod is the connection from that bus down to earth. Earth is not a normal return path: a ground rod typically presents tens to hundreds of ohms — far too high for the hundreds of amps a breaker needs to trip. Current returns to the utility transformer through the metallic neutral.
